title: The Strand Where Files Wash Ashore

features.name: file-shells
features.desc-on-touch: fragments of corrupted downloads, hardened into shapes like shells. you can read the filenames but not what they contained

features.name: tide-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a depression in the shore holding clear water—but the water isn't sea water, it's pure metadata, still, waiting to be read

features.name: beacon-structure
features.desc-on-touch: a latticed tower of fragments, broadcasting the last known signal of each file that washed up here. some are still humming

features.name: soil-merge
features.desc-on-touch: where the digital stops and the earth begins, or maybe where they became the same thing. soil here is slightly crunchy, slightly legible

inscription: STATUS: READING CONTINUES—files wash ashore and the sand learns to read what it's holding

file.name: Strand_Debris_Manifest_Latest.csv
file.note: an inventory of everything that's washed up. new items appear on the list before they arrive at the shore

exits.label: back into the diagnostic water, toward the harbour
inland, toward the village that downloads from this beach
along the coast, toward other strands where other archives wash ashore
